stories.title: 卖不掉的小雕刻
stories.source_range: inspired by early selling attempts and market disappointment scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草这一次带了三件小雕刻去市场。
sentences.english_helper: This time, Weed brings three small carvings to the market.

sentences.chinese: 他比上次准备得更早。
sentences.english_helper: He prepares earlier than last time.

sentences.chinese: 位置也选得更好。
sentences.english_helper: He also chooses a better spot.

sentences.chinese: 他把东西摆得很整齐。
sentences.english_helper: He arranges the pieces very neatly.

sentences.chinese: 然后站在旁边等。
sentences.english_helper: Then he stands beside them and waits.

sentences.chinese: 一开始，他心里还有一点希望。
sentences.english_helper: At first, he still has a little hope in his heart.

sentences.chinese: 可是看的人虽然有，买的人却没有。
sentences.english_helper: But although some people look, no one buys.

sentences.chinese: 有人只看一眼就走。
sentences.english_helper: Some people take one glance and leave.

sentences.chinese: 还有人问了价格以后，就像听见了什么笑话。
sentences.english_helper: Others ask the price and then react as if they heard a joke.

sentences.chinese: 杂草的脸没有变。
sentences.english_helper: Weed's face does not change.

sentences.chinese: 可是心里越来越沉。
sentences.english_helper: But inside, he feels heavier and heavier.

sentences.chinese: 后来，他开始想，也许只是价格太高。
sentences.english_helper: Later, he begins to think that maybe the price is simply too high.

sentences.chinese: 所以他把价格降了一点。
sentences.english_helper: So he lowers the price a little.

sentences.chinese: 过了一会儿，还是没有人买。
sentences.english_helper: After a while, still no one buys.

sentences.chinese: 他又降了一点。
sentences.english_helper: He lowers it again.

sentences.chinese: 那三件东西还是一直摆着。
sentences.english_helper: Those three pieces still remain sitting there on display.

sentences.chinese: 这时候，杂草心里冒出一个很难听的想法。
sentences.english_helper: At this point, a harsh thought rises in Weed's mind.

sentences.chinese: 也许不是价格的问题。
sentences.english_helper: Maybe it is not a price problem.

sentences.chinese: 也许是这些东西根本不值得卖。
sentences.english_helper: Maybe these pieces simply are not worth selling at all.

sentences.chinese: 他差一点就想把它们收起来。
sentences.english_helper: He almost wants to pack them up.

sentences.chinese: 可是一个老人走过来，拿起其中一件，看了很久。
sentences.english_helper: But an old man walks over, picks one up, and looks at it for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 老人最后没有买。
sentences.english_helper: In the end, the old man does not buy it.

sentences.chinese: 老人只问：“这是你自己做的吗？”
sentences.english_helper: The old man only asks, 'Did you make this yourself?'

sentences.chinese: 杂草点头。
sentences.english_helper: Weed nods.

sentences.chinese: 老人又问：“你为什么做这个样子？”
sentences.english_helper: The old man asks again, 'Why did you make it look like this?'

sentences.chinese: 杂草一下子说不出来。
sentences.english_helper: Weed suddenly cannot answer.

sentences.chinese: 因为他刚才只想着卖掉它。
sentences.english_helper: Because a moment ago, he was only thinking about selling it.

sentences.chinese: 他没有想过怎么解释它。
sentences.english_helper: He had not thought about how to explain it.

sentences.chinese: 老人把东西放回去，说：“价格不是最先要想的事。”
sentences.english_helper: The old man puts the piece back and says, 'Price is not the first thing to think about.'

sentences.chinese: “你要先知道，自己做的是什么。”
sentences.english_helper: 'First you need to know what it is that you made.'

sentences.chinese: 老人走了以后，杂草没有再降价格。
sentences.english_helper: After the old man leaves, Weed does not lower the price again.

sentences.chinese: 他只是站在那里，看着自己的三件作品。
sentences.english_helper: He only stands there, looking at his three pieces.

sentences.chinese: 那天他还是没有卖掉。
sentences.english_helper: That day, he still does not sell them.

sentences.chinese: 可是回去的时候，他第一次不是只想“怎么卖”，而是开始想“我到底做了什么”。
sentences.english_helper: But on the way back, for the first time he is no longer only thinking, 'How do I sell this?' He starts thinking, 'What did I actually make?'

stories.title: 改了三次的笑脸
stories.source_range: inspired by repeated carving practice and expression study scenes

sentences.chinese: 杂草那天想做一个笑着的小人。
sentences.english_helper: That day, Weed wants to make a little figure with a smile.

sentences.chinese: 他觉得笑脸看起来很简单。
sentences.english_helper: He thinks a smiling face looks simple.

sentences.chinese: 嘴角往上一点，不就可以了吗？
sentences.english_helper: You just lift the corners of the mouth a little, right?

sentences.chinese: 可是第一件做出来的时候，他自己先皱了眉。
sentences.english_helper: But when the first piece is done, he frowns first himself.

sentences.chinese: 那个小人像是在忍痛。
sentences.english_helper: The little figure looks like it is enduring pain.

sentences.chinese: 一点也不像在笑。
sentences.english_helper: It does not look like it is smiling at all.

sentences.chinese: 杂草不高兴，又做第二次。
sentences.english_helper: Weed is unhappy and makes it a second time.

sentences.chinese: 第二次的嘴更开。
sentences.english_helper: The mouth is more open in the second one.

sentences.chinese: 可是眼睛却很僵。
sentences.english_helper: But the eyes are very stiff.

sentences.chinese: 看起来还是不对。
sentences.english_helper: It still does not look right.

sentences.chinese: 杂草本来以为，只要形状像，东西就会像。
sentences.english_helper: Weed had thought that as long as the shape looked right, the thing itself would look right.

sentences.chinese: 现在他才发现，笑不是只有嘴。
sentences.english_helper: Now he realizes that a smile is not only the mouth.

sentences.chinese: 眼睛也要笑。
sentences.english_helper: The eyes also need to smile.

sentences.chinese: 脸也要放松。
sentences.english_helper: The face also has to relax.

sentences.chinese: 他坐在那里，看着前面的失败品。
sentences.english_helper: He sits there looking at the failed pieces in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 过了一会儿，他拿来一面小镜子。
sentences.english_helper: After a while, he brings over a small mirror.

sentences.chinese: 他先看自己的脸。
sentences.english_helper: He first looks at his own face.

sentences.chinese: 然后试着笑一下。
sentences.english_helper: Then he tries smiling a little.

sentences.chinese: 第一次笑得很奇怪。
sentences.english_helper: The first smile looks strange.

sentences.chinese: 第二次也不太自然。
sentences.english_helper: The second one is also not very natural.

sentences.chinese: 后来，他想到妹妹高兴的时候的样子。
sentences.english_helper: Later, he thinks of how his younger sister looks when she is happy.

sentences.chinese: 又想到奶奶轻轻笑的时候。
sentences.english_helper: Then he thinks of his grandma smiling softly.

sentences.chinese: 他忽然明白，自己不能只靠记住线。
sentences.english_helper: He suddenly understands that he cannot rely only on remembering lines.

sentences.chinese: 自己还要观察人。
sentences.english_helper: He also has to observe people.

sentences.chinese: 第三次下刀的时候，他慢了很多。
sentences.english_helper: When he cuts for the third time, he is much slower.

sentences.chinese: 他先做眼睛。
sentences.english_helper: He shapes the eyes first.

sentences.chinese: 后来才做嘴。
sentences.english_helper: Only later does he shape the mouth.

sentences.chinese: 做完以后，那张脸还是很简单。
sentences.english_helper: After finishing, the face is still very simple.

sentences.chinese: 可是这一次，看起来终于像真的在笑。
sentences.english_helper: But this time, it finally looks like it is really smiling.

sentences.chinese: 杂草看了很久，自己也轻轻笑了一下。
sentences.english_helper: Weed looks at it for a long time and also gives a small smile himself.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，有些东西不能只靠手学。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that some things cannot be learned only with the hands.

sentences.chinese: 有些东西，要先学会看。
sentences.english_helper: Some things must first be learned by watching.
